title: Avoid full rollout on fernet/credential key rotation
description:
# GitHub Issue #206: Stop triggering a full Deployment rollout on every fernet/credential key rotation

**Category**: improvement | **Scope**: Small

**Description**: `reconcileDeployment` (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go:67-74) reads the `fernet-keys` and `credential-keys` Secrets, computes a SHA-256 of their `Data` map, and injects the digests as pod template annotations `keystone.c5c3.io/fernet-keys-hash` and `keystone.c5c3.io/credential-keys-hash` (reconcile_deployment.go:170-173). Because the rotation CronJobs (CC-0013, CC-0036) PATCH those Secrets on every run, the annotation changes, the pod template hash changes, and the entire Keystone Deployment is rolled. Remove the hash plumbing so that key rotation no longer triggers a rollout: drop `keysHash`/`fernetKeysHash`/`credentialKeysHash` (lines 33-61), drop the two annotations from `buildKeystoneDeployment`, and let the kubelet's Secret projection deliver the rotated keys to running pods through their existing volume mounts (`/etc/keystone/fernet-keys/`, `/etc/keystone/credential-keys/`).

**Motivation**: Keystone's fernet provider reads keys from the on-disk keys repository on each token operation, and Kubernetes Secret volume projections are updated in-place within roughly one kubelet sync period after the Secret changes. With `maxActiveKeys` defaulting to 3, the upstream multi-key overlap window already covers any brief skew between pods. The current design throws away those guarantees: every weekly fernet rotation and every credential rotation forces a full rolling restart of all Keystone replicas (3 by default), which (a) causes a capacity dip on what is the gating dependency for every other OpenStack service, (b) breaks long-running uWSGI connections and warm Memcached token caches, (c) multiplies the blast radius of any unrelated bug surfaced during a rollout, and (d) competes for PDB budget with legitimate voluntary disruptions like node drains. Rotation should be a transparent on-disk update, not a fleet-wide restart.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go`
- `tests/e2e/keystone/fernet-rotation/`
- `tests/e2e/keystone/credential-rotation/`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `keysHash`, `fernetKeysHash`, `credentialKeysHash` and their callers in `reconcileDeployment` are removed
- [ ] `buildKeystoneDeployment` no longer emits the `keystone.c5c3.io/fernet-keys-hash` or `keystone.c5c3.io/credential-keys-hash` pod annotations
- [ ] The fernet and credential key Secrets remain mounted as volumes; no behavioural change to in-pod key paths
- [ ] Existing `TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_*HashAnnotation`, `TestFernetKeysHash_*`, `TestCredentialKeysHash_*` and `TestReconcileDeployment_*KeysHashFromSecret` tests are removed or replaced with assertions that the annotations are absent
- [ ] A new unit test asserts that `buildKeystoneDeployment` produces a stable pod template hash across two invocations with different Secret contents
- [ ] The `fernet-rotation` and `credential-rotation` Chainsaw E2E tests are extended to assert that pod UIDs are unchanged after a rotation cycle (no rollout occurred)
- [ ] An E2E assertion proves a token issued before rotation still validates after rotation, demonstrating the multi-key overlap window covers the kubelet projection delay
- [ ] `architecture/docs/09-implementation/04-keystone-reconciler.md` is updated to document that key rotation is in-place and does not roll the Deployment

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/206

stories.title: Operator sees no pod restarts during scheduled key rotation
stories.want: fernet and credential key rotations to update keys in-place via kubelet Secret projection without triggering a Deployment rollout
stories.so_that: Keystone availability is not degraded during routine key rotation, PDB budget is preserved for legitimate disruptions, and uWSGI connections and Memcached caches are not disrupted
criteria: Pod UIDs remain unchanged after a fernet key rotation cycle
Pod UIDs remain unchanged after a credential key rotation cycle
Keystone CR status remains Ready=True throughout rotation
No new ReplicaSet is created by the Deployment controller after rotation

stories.title: Operator verifies tokens remain valid across fernet rotation boundary
stories.want: a fernet token issued before rotation to still validate after rotation completes and kubelet projects the new keys
stories.so_that: I can confirm the multi-key overlap window covers the kubelet projection delay and no token invalidation occurs
criteria: A token obtained via POST /v3/auth/tokens before rotation returns 200 on validation after rotation
The fernet-keys Secret data changes after rotation (rotation actually occurred)
At least 10 seconds elapse between rotation and validation to cover kubelet sync period

stories.title: Developer removes hash annotation plumbing without breaking deployment spec
stories.want: the keysHash, fernetKeysHash, and credentialKeysHash functions and their callers removed from reconcileDeployment, and the hash annotations removed from buildKeystoneDeployment
stories.so_that: the Deployment pod template no longer changes when Secret data changes, eliminating the rollout trigger
criteria: keysHash, fernetKeysHash, credentialKeysHash functions are deleted from reconcile_deployment.go
buildKeystoneDeployment no longer accepts hash parameters
Pod template Annotations map does not contain keystone.c5c3.io/fernet-keys-hash or keystone.c5c3.io/credential-keys-hash
Volume mounts for fernet-keys and credential-keys are unchanged
Unused imports (crypto/sha256, encoding/hex, encoding/json, apierrors, types) are removed

stories.title: Developer verifies pod template stability with unit tests
stories.want: unit tests that assert the pod template produced by buildKeystoneDeployment is stable across invocations regardless of Secret contents
stories.so_that: future changes cannot accidentally re-introduce rollout triggers tied to Secret data
criteria: Old hash-related tests (TestFernetKeysHash_*, TestCredentialKeysHash_*, TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_*HashAnnotation, TestReconcileDeployment_*KeysHashFromSecret) are removed
A new test asserts buildKeystoneDeployment produces identical pod template specs across two invocations
A new test asserts neither fernet-keys-hash nor credential-keys-hash annotations are present in the pod template

stories.title: SRE finds accurate documentation on key rotation behavior
stories.want: architecture and reference documentation to reflect that key rotation is in-place via kubelet Secret projection and does not roll the Deployment
stories.so_that: operational runbooks and incident response procedures are based on current behavior
criteria: architecture/docs/09-implementation/04-keystone-reconciler.md reconcileDeployment section no longer mentions hash annotations or rolling restarts on rotation
docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md Pod Template Annotations table no longer lists fernet-keys-hash or credential-keys-hash
Documentation explains that kubelet Secret projection delivers rotated keys in-place

requirements.description: The reconcileDeployment function SHALL NOT compute SHA-256 hashes of fernet-keys or credential-keys Secrets
requirements.rationale: The hash computation is the root cause of the rollout trigger — removing it eliminates the Deployment spec change on Secret rotation

scenarios.name: Hash functions removed
scenarios.when: the source file reconcile_deployment.go is compiled
scenarios.then: no keysHash, fernetKeysHash, or credentialKeysHash functions exist on KeystoneReconciler
and_then: imports crypto/sha256, encoding/hex, encoding/json are removed

scenarios.name: reconcileDeployment skips hash computation
scenarios.when: reconcileDeployment is called with a Keystone CR and configMapName
scenarios.then: it calls buildKeystoneDeployment with only keystone and configMapName parameters
and_then: no Secret Get calls are made for fernet-keys or credential-keys

scenarios.name: reconcileDeployment tolerates missing secrets
scenarios.when: reconcileDeployment is called and fernet-keys or credential-keys Secrets do not exist
scenarios.then: no error is returned for missing Secret (hash computation path is gone)
and_then: deployment is created normally with volume references to the Secrets

requirements.description: The buildKeystoneDeployment function SHALL NOT emit keystone.c5c3.io/fernet-keys-hash or keystone.c5c3.io/credential-keys-hash pod template annotations
requirements.rationale: These annotations are the mechanism that triggers Kubernetes rolling restarts when Secret data changes

scenarios.name: No fernet-keys-hash annotation
scenarios.when: buildKeystoneDeployment is called with any Keystone CR
scenarios.then: the returned Deployment pod template does not contain the annotation keystone.c5c3.io/fernet-keys-hash

scenarios.name: No credential-keys-hash annotation
scenarios.when: buildKeystoneDeployment is called with any Keystone CR
scenarios.then: the returned Deployment pod template does not contain the annotation keystone.c5c3.io/credential-keys-hash

scenarios.name: Function signature simplified
scenarios.when: buildKeystoneDeployment is called
scenarios.then: it accepts exactly 2 parameters: keystone *Keystone and configMapName string
and_then: all existing call sites in production and test code are updated

requirements.description: The buildKeystoneDeployment function SHALL preserve the existing fernet-keys and credential-keys Secret volume mounts unchanged
requirements.rationale: The kubelet Secret projection via volume mounts is the mechanism that delivers rotated keys to running pods without restarts

scenarios.name: Fernet keys volume mount preserved
scenarios.when: buildKeystoneDeployment builds the Deployment spec
scenarios.then: a volume mount named fernet-keys with mountPath /etc/keystone/fernet-keys/ (readOnly: true) exists on the container
and_then: a volume named fernet-keys sourcing from Secret {name}-fernet-keys exists

scenarios.name: Credential keys volume mount preserved
scenarios.when: buildKeystoneDeployment builds the Deployment spec
scenarios.then: a volume mount named credential-keys with mountPath /etc/keystone/credential-keys/ (readOnly: true) exists on the container
and_then: a volume named credential-keys sourcing from Secret {name}-credential-keys exists

requirements.description: All existing hash-related unit tests SHALL be removed and replaced with assertions that the annotations are absent
requirements.rationale: Tests that assert hash annotation presence would fail after the code change; replacement tests prevent regression by asserting annotations remain absent

scenarios.name: Hash test functions removed
scenarios.when: the test file reconcile_deployment_test.go is compiled
scenarios.then: no TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_FernetKeysHashAnnotation, TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_CredentialKeysHashAnnotation, TestFernetKeysHash_Deterministic, TestFernetKeysHash_SecretNotFound, TestCredentialKeysHash_Deterministic, TestCredentialKeysHash_SecretNotFound, TestReconcileDeployment_FernetKeysHashFromSecret, or TestReconcileDeployment_CredentialKeysHashFromSecret functions exist
and_then: test helper functions deployTestFernetKeysSecret and deployTestCredentialKeysSecret are removed
unused imports crypto/sha256, encoding/hex, encoding/json, apierrors are removed

scenarios.name: Replacement test asserts annotations absent
scenarios.when: TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_NoHashAnnotations runs
scenarios.then: it verifies the pod template annotations map does not contain keystone.c5c3.io/fernet-keys-hash or keystone.c5c3.io/credential-keys-hash

requirements.description: A unit test SHALL assert that buildKeystoneDeployment produces a stable pod template spec across two invocations
requirements.rationale: Proves that the Deployment spec does not vary between reconciliation loops, preventing unintended rollouts

scenarios.name: Identical outputs across invocations
scenarios.when: buildKeystoneDeployment is called twice with the same Keystone CR and configMapName
scenarios.then: both returned Deployment objects have identical Spec.Template fields

scenarios.name: Output stable regardless of external state
scenarios.when: buildKeystoneDeployment is called at different points in time without any CR changes
scenarios.then: the pod template hash would be identical, causing no Deployment rollout

requirements.description: The fernet-rotation Chainsaw E2E test SHALL assert that pod UIDs are unchanged after a fernet rotation cycle
requirements.rationale: End-to-end proof that fernet key rotation does not trigger a Deployment rollout in a real cluster

scenarios.name: Pod UIDs stable after fernet rotation
scenarios.when: a manual fernet key rotation Job completes and the reconciler processes the Secret change
scenarios.then: all Keystone API pod UIDs are identical to their pre-rotation values
and_then: the Secret data has verifiably changed (rotation occurred)

scenarios.name: No annotation change assertion removed
scenarios.when: the E2E test Step 4 runs
scenarios.then: it no longer polls for or asserts a Deployment annotation change
and_then: the test header comments are updated to reflect the new behavior

requirements.description: The credential-rotation Chainsaw E2E test SHALL assert that pod UIDs are unchanged after a credential rotation cycle
requirements.rationale: End-to-end proof that credential key rotation does not trigger a Deployment rollout in a real cluster

scenarios.name: Pod UIDs stable after credential rotation
scenarios.when: a manual credential key rotation Job completes and the reconciler processes the Secret change
scenarios.then: all Keystone API pod UIDs are identical to their pre-rotation values
and_then: the Secret data has verifiably changed (rotation occurred)

scenarios.name: No annotation change assertion removed
scenarios.when: the E2E test Step 4 runs
scenarios.then: it no longer polls for or asserts a Deployment annotation change
and_then: the test header comments are updated to reflect the new behavior

requirements.description: The fernet-rotation E2E test SHALL assert that a token issued before rotation still validates after rotation
requirements.rationale: Proves the multi-key overlap window covers the kubelet projection delay, demonstrating that in-place rotation is safe for token continuity

scenarios.name: Pre-rotation token validates post-rotation
scenarios.when: a fernet token is obtained via POST /v3/auth/tokens before rotation, then a fernet rotation completes and kubelet projects the new keys
scenarios.then: validating the pre-rotation token via GET /v3/auth/tokens returns HTTP 200
and_then: at least 10 seconds elapse between rotation completion and token validation to cover kubelet sync period

scenarios.name: Rotation actually occurred
scenarios.when: the token validation step runs
scenarios.then: the fernet-keys Secret data hash has changed compared to the pre-rotation snapshot
and_then: the validation proves multi-key overlap, not stale keys

requirements.description: The architecture and reference documentation SHALL be updated to document in-place key rotation without Deployment rollout
requirements.rationale: Documentation must reflect the current behavior to prevent operational confusion and incorrect runbooks

scenarios.name: Architecture doc updated
scenarios.when: an SRE reads architecture/docs/09-implementation/04-keystone-reconciler.md
scenarios.then: the reconcileDeployment section does not mention hash annotations, rolling restarts on rotation, or the CronJob → hash → annotation → rollout flow
and_then: the section explains that key rotation is in-place via kubelet Secret projection

scenarios.name: Reference doc updated
scenarios.when: an SRE reads docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md
scenarios.then: the Pod Template Annotations table does not list fernet-keys-hash or credential-keys-hash
and_then: the Fernet Keys Hash Annotation (CC-0015) section is replaced with an In-Place Key Rotation (CC-0074) section explaining the kubelet projection mechanism

tasks.title: Remove hash functions, callers, and annotations from reconcile_deployment.go (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go: (1) Delete the keysHash function (lines 39-52), fernetKeysHash function (lines 54-58), and credentialKeysHash function (lines 60-64). (2) In reconcileDeployment, remove lines 70-77 (fernetHash/credentialHash computation and error handling) and change the buildKeystoneDeployment call at line 78 from buildKeystoneDeployment(keystone, configMapName, fernetHash, credentialHash) to buildKeystoneDeployment(keystone, configMapName). (3) Change the buildKeystoneDeployment function signature at line 154 from (keystone, configMapName, fernetKeysHash, credentialKeysHash) to (keystone, configMapName). (4) Remove the Annotations field from the pod template ObjectMeta (lines 174-177) — the only annotations are the two hash annotations, so remove the entire Annotations key. (5) Remove unused imports: crypto/sha256, encoding/hex, encoding/json, apierrors (k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors), types (k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types). Verify volume mounts at lines 227-243 and volume definitions at lines 245-272 remain untouched.

tasks.title: Remove hash tests, update call sites, and add no-annotation/stable-template tests (REQ-004, REQ-005)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go: (1) Delete the following test functions and helpers: deployTestFernetKeysSecret (lines 418-429), TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_FernetKeysHashAnnotation (lines 431-441), TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_FernetKeysHashAnnotation_EmptyHash (lines 443-452), deployTestCredentialKeysSecret (lines 456-467), TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_CredentialKeysHashAnnotation (lines 469-479), TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_CredentialKeysHashAnnotation_EmptyHash (lines 481-490), TestCredentialKeysHash_Deterministic (lines 492-522), TestCredentialKeysHash_SecretNotFound (lines 524-535), TestReconcileDeployment_CredentialKeysHashFromSecret (lines 537-563), TestFernetKeysHash_Deterministic (lines 565-595), TestFernetKeysHash_SecretNotFound (lines 597-608), TestReconcileDeployment_FernetKeysHashFromSecret (lines 610-636). (2) Update all remaining buildKeystoneDeployment call sites to use 2-arg signature (remove the trailing "", "" args): readyDeployment helper (line 98), notReadyDeployment helper (line 115), TestReconcileDeployment_ContainerResources (line 809), TestReconcileDeployment_CustomResources (line 833), TestReconcileDeployment_NilResources (line 852). (3) Remove unused imports: crypto/sha256, encoding/hex, encoding/json, apierrors. (4) Add new test TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_NoHashAnnotations: build deployment, assert annotations map is nil or does not contain keystone.c5c3.io/fernet-keys-hash or keystone.c5c3.io/credential-keys-hash. (5) Add new test TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_StablePodTemplate (CC-0074): call buildKeystoneDeployment twice with the same inputs, assert both Spec.Template fields are deeply equal using Gomega Equal matcher. Run go vet and golangci-lint to verify.

tasks.title: Update fernet-rotation E2E test: pod UID stability and token validation (REQ-006, REQ-008)
tasks.description: In tests/e2e/keystone/fernet-rotation/chainsaw-test.yaml: (1) Update header comments (lines 5-12) to reference CC-0074 and describe the new assertions: no rollout after rotation, token validation across rotation boundary. (2) Rewrite Step 4 (lines 79-123) script: (a) Record pod UIDs before rotation using kubectl get pods -n openstack -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=keystone-fernet -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.uid}'. (b) Obtain a fernet token from Keystone before rotation via kubectl exec into a keystone pod running curl POST /v3/auth/tokens with admin credentials from the bootstrap secret, capture the X-Subject-Token header. (c) Trigger manual rotation via kubectl create job --from=cronjob and wait for completion. (d) Verify Secret data changed (existing check). (e) Sleep 15s to allow kubelet Secret projection to complete. (f) Verify pod UIDs unchanged — compare sorted UIDs before and after, fail if different. (g) Validate the pre-rotation token via kubectl exec curl GET /v3/auth/tokens with X-Auth-Token and X-Subject-Token headers, assert HTTP 200. Update the catch block debug output accordingly.

tasks.title: Update credential-rotation E2E test: pod UID stability assertion (REQ-007)
tasks.description: In tests/e2e/keystone/credential-rotation/chainsaw-test.yaml: (1) Update header comments (lines 5-12) to reference CC-0074 and describe the new assertion: no rollout after rotation. (2) Rewrite Step 4 (lines 79-123) script: (a) Record pod UIDs before rotation using kubectl get pods -n openstack -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=keystone-credential -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.uid}'. (b) Trigger manual rotation via kubectl create job --from=cronjob and wait for completion. (c) Verify Secret data changed (existing check). (d) Sleep 15s to allow kubelet Secret projection to complete. (e) Verify pod UIDs unchanged — compare sorted UIDs before and after, fail if different. Remove the annotation polling loop entirely. Update the catch block debug output accordingly.

tasks.title: Update architecture doc for in-place key rotation (REQ-009)
tasks.description: In architecture/docs/09-implementation/04-keystone-reconciler.md: The reconcileDeployment section (starting at line 355) currently shows hash annotations in the code example — it does not include the hash computation code because it shows a simplified pseudocode. No code change needed there. However, if there is any mention of hash-based rollout triggers in this file, remove it and add a note that key rotation is in-place via kubelet Secret projection (CC-0074). Explain that Keystone's fernet provider reads keys from disk on each token operation, and the multi-key overlap window (maxActiveKeys=3) covers the kubelet sync period. Reference CC-0074 in the change.

tasks.title: Update reference doc: remove hash annotation docs, add in-place rotation note (REQ-009)
tasks.description: In docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md: (1) Replace the 'Fernet Keys Hash Annotation (CC-0015)' section (lines 952-973) with an 'In-Place Key Rotation (CC-0074)' section explaining: reconcileDeployment no longer computes hashes or sets hash annotations; key rotation is delivered in-place via kubelet Secret volume projection; Keystone reads keys from disk on each token operation; maxActiveKeys=3 ensures the multi-key overlap window covers kubelet sync delay. (2) Remove the fernet-keys-hash row from the Pod Template Annotations table (lines 987-991) — if the table becomes empty, remove the table and heading. (3) Verify the Volume Mounts table (lines 1002-1008) is unchanged. (4) Remove the fernetKeysHash() helper table (lines 965-973) and the CronJob → hash → annotation flow diagram (lines 959-963).

review_criteria: keysHash, fernetKeysHash, credentialKeysHash functions are completely removed from reconcile_deployment.go — no dead code remains
buildKeystoneDeployment accepts exactly 2 parameters (keystone, configMapName) — no hash parameters
Pod template Annotations field is removed or empty — no keystone.c5c3.io/fernet-keys-hash or credential-keys-hash keys present
Volume mounts for fernet-keys (/etc/keystone/fernet-keys/) and credential-keys (/etc/keystone/credential-keys/) are unchanged in the Deployment spec
All 12 hash-related tests are removed; new TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_NoHashAnnotations and TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_StablePodTemplate tests pass
All 5 non-hash buildKeystoneDeployment call sites (readyDeployment, notReadyDeployment, ContainerResources, CustomResources, NilResources) updated to 2-arg signature
Unused imports removed from both reconcile_deployment.go and reconcile_deployment_test.go — go vet and golangci-lint pass clean
E2E fernet-rotation Step 4 asserts pod UID stability and pre-rotation token validation — annotation polling removed
E2E credential-rotation Step 4 asserts pod UID stability — annotation polling removed
docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md Fernet Keys Hash Annotation section replaced with In-Place Key Rotation section; Pod Template Annotations table updated
implementation_notes:
Architectural decision: Remove the hash-annotation-triggered-rollout mechanism entirely rather than making it configurable. The kubelet Secret projection + multi-key overlap window is the correct design for key rotation in Keystone. The hash mechanism was added in CC-0015 when the rotation CronJobs were first implemented, before the multi-key overlap behavior was well understood.

Import cleanup: After removing the hash functions, reconcile_deployment.go no longer needs crypto/sha256, encoding/hex, encoding/json, apierrors, or types imports. The test file no longer needs crypto/sha256, encoding/hex, encoding/json, or apierrors imports.

One-time rollout: When the updated operator is deployed to existing clusters, the first reconciliation will produce a Deployment without the hash annotations. This is a one-time spec change that will trigger a single rollout. After that, no further rotation-triggered rollouts will occur. This is acceptable and expected.

E2E token validation approach: Use kubectl exec into a Keystone pod to curl the local Keystone API. The admin password is available from the keystone-admin-credentials Secret. The token lifecycle is: POST /v3/auth/tokens (get token) → rotate keys → GET /v3/auth/tokens with X-Auth-Token + X-Subject-Token (validate token).

Potential pitfall: The buildKeystoneDeployment function currently initializes the Annotations map only for hash values. After removal, the pod template ObjectMeta will have no Annotations field (nil). Ensure no other code depends on the Annotations map being non-nil.
